rows.notes: Clear centred page title at top. Handwriting legible for the main words 'Recepta Anni Presentis' though spelling is archaic; no monetary columns clearly aligned to this line.

rows.notes: A subtotal aligned to the right of the parish list. The three columns read clearly as 63 (pounds), 3 (shillings), 4 (pence). No descriptive text on the same horizontal level; likely a subtotal for the preceding list.

Large total line beneath a ruled separator. The left two columns read '99' and '10' clearly. The pence column looks like '4' followed by a small loop-mark that resembles the common halfpenny 'ob' flourish; I interpret this as 4Â½d rather than '49' pence (which would be invalid). Reading of the loop/fraction is moderately uncertain due to scribble.
Small marginal note appearing under the section divider; reads 'More: Sr Batt:' (very uncertain). No amounts on this line; ink is faint and partially obscured by a shadow.
rows.english_description: Additional receipts recorded from Sir Batt (Sir Batte).
